methods.description: Process for preparing and observing pollen grains using light microscopy (LM) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM) to analyze their morphological characteristics.

steps.description: Plant specimens were collected and voucher samples were deposited at the Mardin Artuklu University herbarium, Turkey.
steps.searchable_summary: collect plant specimens and deposit voucher samples in herbarium

steps.description: Pollen grains for LM examination were prepared following the standard procedure of Wodehouse (1935).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen grains for light microscopy using Wodehouse method

steps.description: Pollen grains for SEM were removed by distilled water treatment, air-dried, and directly mounted on stubs using double-sided adhesive tape and uncoated.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen grains for SEM by water treatment and mounting

steps.description: The photomicrographs were taken with a FEI Quanta Feg 250 scanning electron microscope.

methods.description: Measurement and statistical analysis of pollen grain characteristics such as shape, size, and thickness using light microscopy.

steps.description: Pollen shape, size, ornamentation, polar length, equatorial width, exine and intine thickness, colpus length and colpus width for 30 pollen grains were measured under binocular light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen grain characteristics using light microscope

steps.description: For average, five readings were taken and statistically analyzed by using IBM SPSS statistics 24 software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis on pollen measurements using SPSS
